originalBehavior: The route combines a top-level article page with an iframe-hosted interactive animation so descriptive reading and the canvas runtime stay on one page.
localStatus: The split article-and-iframe structure is preserved, with the public article body reduced to neutral framing, educational links, and the shared footer.
notes: The interactive still loads through the original iframe path instead of being folded into a new app shell.
Public creator, translation, and share sections were removed from the article body without changing the iframe runtime.
evidence: Local route walkthrough on 2026-03-10 confirmed the page still opens with the same iframe-first layout.
Source orientation in compilations/ncase-neurons.md treats the mixed article-plus-interactive shell as a parity-critical part of the experience.

originalBehavior: Early scenes let users click neurons, watch signals propagate, and transition from a generic firing model into anxiety-linked trigger pathways.
localStatus: Propagation and trigger-response scenes remain in the original runtime, with the intro narration now starting after the self-introduction voice segment.
notes: The click-to-fire interaction, scene interrupts, and progression into the anxiety map remain upstream-driven.
Skipping the first spoken self-intro lines avoids public creator callouts without altering the click-gated narrative flow.
evidence: Smoke review targeted the first click interrupts and propagation sequence because they anchor the entire route.
Narrator state transitions still use the original INTRO, PROP_INTERRUPTABLE, and FEAR sequence.

originalBehavior: Users build new neural links by firing neurons together, visually strengthening or creating connections through a guided Hebbian-learning chapter.
localStatus: The Hebbian chapter remains in the original scene and narrator runtime, with no local rewrite of the connection-building rules.
notes: Connection creation, highlighting, and progression checks still depend on the upstream neuron runtime.
The local port preserved the guided challenge structure instead of flattening it into a free sandbox immediately.
evidence: Parity scope includes the Hebbian lesson because it is the route's first major teach-by-doing mechanic.
Source files still isolate Hebbian narration and scene logic as separate upstream modules.

originalBehavior: A later chapter weakens existing connections when users fire neurons out of sync, demonstrating anti-Hebbian unlearning through repeated interaction.
localStatus: Anti-Hebbian behavior remains intact in the original scene and narrator stack, sharing the same neuron and connection engine as upstream.
notes: The route still requires users to find valid outgoing connections and wait through weakening cycles.
Local cleanup avoided changing the anti-Hebbian state machine or chapter timing.
evidence: Live smoke testing confirmed connection weakening and deletion still trigger the next scene transitions.
The upstream narrator and scene files continue to coordinate the anti-Hebbian lesson without host-page intervention.

originalBehavior: The therapy section asks users to retrain an anxious network, then transitions into a calmer outro and optional extra scene once the rewiring task is complete.
localStatus: Therapy, rewiring, and the outro remain in the original anxiety and story scenes, using the same win-condition and state transitions as upstream.
notes: Rewiring still depends on the same trigger-response graph rather than a simplified static retelling.
The local article copy mentions the sandbox and further reading, but the therapy progression itself is unchanged.
evidence: Parity targets in compilations/ncase-neurons.md call out therapy and trigger-response scenes as core interactions.
Source files still split anxiety, therapy, and outro logic across the original scene modules.

originalBehavior: The route ends with a credits sequence, keeps caption and playback controls available, and lets users replay or revisit the sandbox after the guided story.
localStatus: Playback and captions controls remain intact, while the credits chapter now uses only the later neutral segment of the original audio and no longer opens external credit cards.
notes: The shipped captions bundle was reduced to English only so public JS no longer carries translation-credit or Patreon strings.
The finale scene still plays, but the clickable sponsor and upstream-link cards were removed from the public route.
evidence: Smoke review targeted the end-of-route controls and credits progression because that is where most public-surface cleanup was required.
The recheck plan explicitly called out footer-only references and narration cleanup for the new explorable article route.
